zotero.abstractNote: Deep learning-based super-resolution (SR) models are data-hungry and require large-scale high-resolution (HR) datasets for training. However, acquiring HR images is expensive and time-consuming. Active learning (AL) is a promising paradigm to reduce the annotation burden by selecting the most informative samples for training. Existing AL methods for SR are typically image-level, selecting entire images for annotation. In this work, we propose a novel dense active learning framework for SR, termed USIM-DAL, which selects informative patches within images for annotation. We introduce a novel uncertainty estimation technique based on statistical image modeling (SIM) that captures the local image statistics to identify regions where the model is uncertain. Our method is model-agnostic and can be integrated with any SR architecture. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmark datasets, showing that USIM-DAL achieves competitive performance with significantly fewer training samples compared to state-of-the-art AL methods.
zotero.conferenceName: Thirty-Ninth Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence

extra.Editors: Evans, Robin J. and Shpitser, Ilya

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the Thirty-Ninth Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence
